name: Baingan Bharta
tagline: Smoke-charred eggplant mashed with onion, tomato, and green chili
highlight: Open-flame char gives the dish its smoky body — no shortcut replicates it

hero.alt: Smoky charred eggplant mash with onion, green chili, and coriander in a kadhai
hero.caption: baingan bharta — smoky, soft, fiber-rich

healthFacts: Eggplant is rich in nasunin, an anthocyanin antioxidant that protects cell membranes.
High-potassium, high-fiber, very low calorie — pairs well with rotis without driving the glucose load up.
Skin-on cooking preserves most of the polyphenol content.
No cream or cashew base — one of the leanest North Indian curries.

steps.title: Char the eggplant
steps.detail: Rub the eggplant with a teaspoon of oil. Roast directly on a gas flame, turning every 2 minutes, until the skin is fully blackened and the inside is soft (12–15 min). Cool, peel off the charred skin, and mash with a fork.
steps.tip: Don't skip this — oven roasting works but loses 70% of the smoke flavor.

steps.title: Sauté the masala
steps.detail: Heat oil in a kadhai, crackle cumin. Add onion and green chili, cook till translucent. Add ginger-garlic and tomato, cook 6 minutes until tomato breaks down.
steps.tip: Mustard oil gives the bharta its classic North Indian profile — heat it till smoking before adding cumin.

steps.title: Combine and simmer
steps.detail: Tip in the mashed eggplant and salt. Stir-cook on low for 8 minutes — moisture should evaporate and oil should glisten back at the edges. Finish with chopped coriander.
steps.tip: Eat hot with phulka or jowar roti — bharta loses its perfume at room temperature.
